title: Build an anchored size ladder of best-reach subsets across k
summary: Build an anchored size ladder: search for the best subset at the anchor size and grow/shrink one item at a time to produce the value curve. Writes the anchored ladder, refined sizes, and prepared input to out_dir.
description: Searches for the best subset at a chosen anchor size and then walks up and / or down by one item at a time to produce a complete value-vs-size curve. The ladder reuses anchor candidates as starting points for adjacent sizes, which is dramatically faster than running an independent search at each size. Specific sizes can also be refined with a heavier search method after the cheap ladder pass.
usage:
[["maxdiff-turf::search-ladder"]]
utilities = "<utilities.csv>"
out_dir = "<dir>"
anchor_k = <int>
# optional:
subgroups = "<subgroups.csv>"
labels = "<labels.csv>"
include = "1,4"
exclude = "2,3"
utility_cols = "3-12"
non_utility_cols = "1,2"
forbidden_pairs = "1:2,3:4"
reach_method = "weighted-by-probability"
anchor_search_method = "auto"
anchor_top_n = 25
ladder_mode = "repair"
direction = "both"
k_min = 1
k_max = 20
beam_width = 25
step_top_n = 10
refine_sizes = [5, 8]
refine_method = "sampled"
seed = 42

arguments.name: utilities
arguments.description: Path to a per-respondent utilities CSV. Same format as every other maxdiff-turf command: one row per respondent, one column per item (after `non_utility_cols` are stripped).

arguments.name: out_dir
arguments.description: Output directory for results. Created if it does not exist. Receives the anchored ladder CSV, any refined-size CSVs, and a prepared-input snapshot.

arguments.name: anchor_k
arguments.description: Anchor subset size used to seed the ladder. The ladder starts here, then walks down to `k_min` and / or up to `k_max` from this point. Pick `anchor_k` near the size you care most about — quality is highest at the anchor and degrades smoothly outward.

arguments.name: subgroups
arguments.description: Path to an optional subgroup-membership CSV. When supplied, per-subgroup reach is reported alongside the overall reach at every step of the ladder.

arguments.name: labels
arguments.description: Path to an optional item-labels CSV used to render ladder steps in human-readable form.

arguments.name: include
arguments.description: Comma-separated 1-based item indices to force-include at every ladder step. Locked-in items count toward the per-step `k`.

arguments.name: exclude
arguments.description: Comma-separated 1-based item indices to exclude from the entire ladder.

arguments.name: utility_cols
arguments.description: Explicit utility column spec, 1-based, supports ranges like `3-12`.

arguments.name: non_utility_cols
arguments.description: Comma-separated 1-based columns to ignore (id, weight, demographic columns).

arguments.name: forbidden_pairs
arguments.description: Forbidden-pair spec, e.g. `1:2,3:4`. Enforced at every ladder step.

arguments.name: headers
arguments.description: Whether the input CSVs have header rows. Default `true`.

arguments.name: reach_method
arguments.description: Reach scoring rule used at every ladder step. See `search-k` for the per-method details.

arguments.name: number_of_alt
arguments.description: Number of alternatives in the implied choice task used by the reach calculation. Default `5`.

arguments.name: threshold
arguments.description: Threshold value for the threshold reach method. Ignored unless `reach_method = "threshold"`.

arguments.name: anchor_search_method
arguments.description: Search strategy used at the anchor size. Same semantics as `search-k`'s `search_method`. The anchor pass is the most expensive step in the ladder; pick a method that gives high-quality candidates because the rest of the ladder builds on them.

arguments.name: anchor_exhaustive_limit
arguments.description: Anchor exhaustive enumeration limit. If `C(n, anchor_k)` exceeds this, `auto` falls back to a non-exhaustive method at the anchor.

arguments.name: anchor_top_n
arguments.description: Top-N candidates retained at the anchor size. These become the seeds for the ladder traversal — larger values explore more of the local optimum landscape at proportional cost.

arguments.name: ladder_mode
arguments.description: Ladder construction mode. `repair` (default) allows the local-improvement pass to swap items as the ladder grows / shrinks, repairing any sub-optimal step. `strict` only adds or removes the single best item at each step. `beam` uses a beam-search step internally.

arguments.name: direction
arguments.description: Direction(s) of ladder traversal from the anchor. `both` (default) walks up and down. `up` only grows; `down` only shrinks.

arguments.name: k_min
arguments.description: Minimum subset size visited along the ladder. Default `1`. The traversal stops here when walking down.

arguments.name: k_max
arguments.description: Maximum subset size visited along the ladder. Defaults to the count of all eligible items. The traversal stops here when walking up.

arguments.name: beam_width
arguments.description: Beam width during ladder traversal when `ladder_mode = "beam"`. Default `25` is fine for most problems.

arguments.name: step_top_n
arguments.description: Number of candidates kept per ladder step. Larger values give a richer per-step diagnostic but only the top one feeds the next step's seeding.

arguments.name: local_improve
arguments.description: When `true`, apply a local-improvement pass at every ladder step. Default `true`.

arguments.name: local_improve_max_iter
arguments.description: Max iterations for local improvement at each step.

arguments.name: refine_sizes
arguments.description: Subset sizes to refine after the initial ladder pass. For each size in the list, a heavier search (per `refine_method`) is run with the ladder result as a starting point. Use this when you only need top-quality answers at a few specific sizes — typically the ones that will appear in a stakeholder report — rather than along the entire curve.

arguments.name: refine_method
arguments.description: Search method used during refine passes on `refine_sizes`.

arguments.name: sample_n
arguments.description: Sampled-search budget for the anchor and refine passes. Overrides `sample_factor` when set.

arguments.name: sample_factor
arguments.description: Multiplier on the auto-sampled budget when `sample_n` is unset. Default `20`.

arguments.name: sample_replicates
arguments.description: Sampled-search replicates. Default `3`.

arguments.name: drop_frac
arguments.description: Fraction of items dropped each screening pass during sampled search. Default `0.25`.

arguments.name: min_survivors
arguments.description: Minimum survivors retained during screening rounds.

arguments.name: screen_metric
arguments.description: Screening metric used during sampled search. Default `hybrid`.

arguments.name: top_alpha
arguments.description: Top-alpha tail probability for the `top-rate` and `hybrid` screening metrics. Default `0.10`.

arguments.name: seed
arguments.description: Random seed for reproducibility. Threaded through the anchor search, ladder traversal, and refine passes.

arguments.name: keep_scenarios
arguments.description: Retain per-respondent scenario rows. Default `true`.

arguments.name: keep_diagnostics
arguments.description: Retain diagnostic columns at every ladder step. Default `true`.

arguments.name: target_subgroup
arguments.description: Name of a single subgroup column to optimise against, instead of overall reach.

arguments.name: threads
arguments.description: Number of Rayon worker threads used to score candidate subsets in parallel at each ladder step (anchor search, step add/drop scoring, beam expansion, and any refine passes). `1` (default) runs serially. Results are deterministic under a given `seed` regardless of thread count.

details:
`search-ladder` is the standard way to produce a value curve — reach as a function of subset size — for a single dataset. The naive approach of running `search-k` once per size is wasteful: most of the work at size `k+1` is the same work as at size `k`, plus one item. The ladder exploits this by anchoring at a single size and then walking outward, reusing anchor candidates as warm starts for adjacent sizes.

The algorithm runs in three phases:

1. **Anchor.** A full search at `anchor_k` using `anchor_search_method`. This is the most expensive single step and produces `anchor_top_n` seed candidates.
2. **Ladder traversal.** Starting from the anchor, walk one item at a time toward `k_min` and / or `k_max` (controlled by `direction`). At each step, evaluate item additions / removals against each retained candidate, apply `local_improve`, and keep `step_top_n` survivors.
3. **Refine (optional).** For each size listed in `refine_sizes`, run a heavier search using `refine_method` with the ladder's result as a starting point. This is useful when you only need high-quality answers at a few specific sizes — typically the ones that will appear in a report — rather than along the full curve.

`ladder_mode = "repair"` is the default and is a good middle ground: it allows the local-improvement pass to repair sub-optimal steps but does not run a full search at every size. Use `"beam"` when you want a deterministic, reproducible traversal, or `"strict"` for the simplest single-item step at each rung.
value:
On success, `out_dir` contains:

- The anchored ladder CSV: one row per (k, candidate) at every ladder step from `k_min` to `k_max` (limited by `direction`), with the reach score and any per-respondent / per-subgroup columns requested.
- One refined CSV per size listed in `refine_sizes`, if any.
- A snapshot of the prepared input recording resolved utility columns, applied filters, and ladder settings.

In protocol mode the plugin emits `event.run.started`, one `event.artifact.created` per file written, and a final `event.run.finished` (or `event.run.failed`).
notes: Quality of the ladder is highest at the anchor and degrades smoothly outward. Pick `anchor_k` near the size you care most about.
If you need a *family* of ladders (different anchors for sensitivity analysis), use `curve-family` instead — it parallelises the per-anchor work.
`refine_sizes` is the right tool when only a handful of specific sizes need top-quality answers; running the entire ladder under a heavy method is usually wasteful.

see_also.description: Single-size search — useful when you only need one k.
Run anchored ladders from multiple anchor sizes in parallel.
Score a fixed list of hand-built subsets at any size.

examples.title: Down-only ladder with refine on the 5- and 8-item sizes
examples.body:
[["maxdiff-turf::search-ladder"]]
utilities = "./utilities.csv"
out_dir = "./ladder"
anchor_k = 12
direction = "down"
k_min = 3
refine_sizes = [5, 8]
refine_method = "sampled"
seed = 42

fields.name: utilities
fields.description: Path to per-respondent utilities CSV.

fields.name: out_dir
fields.description: Output directory for results. Created if it does not exist.

fields.name: anchor_k
fields.description: Anchor subset size used to seed the ladder.

fields.name: subgroups
fields.description: Optional subgroup membership CSV.

fields.name: labels
fields.description: Optional item labels CSV.

fields.name: include
fields.description: Comma-separated 1-based item indices to force-include.

fields.name: exclude
fields.description: Comma-separated 1-based item indices to exclude.

fields.name: utility_cols
fields.description: Explicit utility column spec (1-based, supports ranges).

fields.name: non_utility_cols
fields.description: Comma-separated 1-based columns to ignore.

fields.name: forbidden_pairs
fields.description: Forbidden pairs spec, e.g. '1:2,3:4'.

fields.name: headers
fields.description: Whether the CSV files have header rows. Default: true.

fields.name: reach_method
fields.description: Reach scoring rule.

fields.name: number_of_alt
fields.description: Implied alternatives per task. Default: 5.

fields.name: threshold
fields.description: Threshold value for the threshold reach method. Default: 0.8.

fields.name: anchor_search_method
fields.description: Search strategy used at the anchor size.

fields.name: anchor_exhaustive_limit
fields.description: Anchor exhaustive enumeration limit. Default: 100000.

fields.name: anchor_top_n
fields.description: Top-N candidates retained at the anchor size. Default: 25.

fields.name: ladder_mode
fields.description: Ladder construction mode.

fields.name: direction
fields.description: Direction(s) of ladder traversal from the anchor.

fields.name: k_min
fields.description: Minimum subset size visited along the ladder. Default: 1.

fields.name: k_max
fields.description: Maximum subset size; defaults to all eligible items.

fields.name: beam_width
fields.description: Beam width during ladder traversal. Default: 25.

fields.name: step_top_n
fields.description: Candidates kept per ladder step. Default: 10.

fields.name: local_improve
fields.description: Apply local improvement pass to candidates. Default: true.

fields.name: local_improve_max_iter
fields.description: Max iterations for local improvement. Default: 25.

fields.name: refine_sizes
fields.description: Subset sizes to refine after the initial ladder (array of integers).

fields.name: refine_method
fields.description: Search method used during refine passes.

fields.name: sample_n
fields.description: Sampled-search budget.

fields.name: sample_factor
fields.description: Multiplier on the auto-sampled budget. Default: 20.

fields.name: sample_replicates
fields.description: Sampled-search replicates. Default: 3.

fields.name: drop_frac
fields.description: Fraction of items dropped each screening pass. Default: 0.25.

fields.name: min_survivors
fields.description: Minimum survivors retained during screening rounds.

fields.name: screen_metric
fields.description: Screening metric used during sampled search.

fields.name: top_alpha
fields.description: Top-alpha tail probability for screening. Default: 0.10.

fields.name: seed
fields.description: Random seed (omit for non-deterministic).

fields.name: keep_scenarios
fields.description: Retain per-respondent scenario rows. Default: true.

fields.name: keep_diagnostics
fields.description: Retain diagnostic columns. Default: true.

fields.name: target_subgroup
fields.description: Optimize against a specific subgroup column.

fields.name: threads
fields.description: Rayon worker threads for parallel candidate scoring. Default: 1.
